symptoms.symptom_name: Relentless inconsolable crying (6 hours) unrelieved by comfort measures or paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In a pre-verbal infant, unremitting inconsolable crying is the principal signal of severe pain from an underlying acute cause.
Colic cannot explain the accompanying objective findings (dusky cool toes, cast odour, leg swelling) and is a diagnosis of exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dusky/purple, cool, swollen toes distal to the right cast (compared with warm pink left toes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colour change, coolness and swelling of digits distal to a circumferential cast indicate compromised perfusion and are a limb-threatening emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Foul rotten-meat odour from the proximal opening of the right cast

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Infected pressure ulcer under the cast
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A foul odour from a cast is a classic sign of wound breakdown and infection/necrosis of the skin beneath the plaster.

symptoms.symptom_name: Swelling of the lower leg just above the proximal cast edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Swelling above a tight cast supports impaired venous return and a cast that has become too constrictive.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (approx 4 seconds) in the right toes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged capillary refill objectively confirms impaired distal perfusion; it is a clinician-elicited sign, not reliably obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stage III pressure ulcer (3x3 cm) over the medial malleolus with purulent exudate and surrounding erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Infected pressure ulcer under the cast
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly visualised only after emergency cast removal — sealed clinician finding; not visible to the AI while the cast is on.

symptoms.symptom_name: Infant (5 weeks, 4.8 kg) in week 3 of serial long-leg casting for bilateral clubfoot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serial casting over bony prominences in a rapidly growing infant is the exposure that creates pressure-ulcer and cast-compression risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 170), diaphoresis and low-grade temperature (37.8 C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic signs of severe pain and possible early infection; precise HR/BP are clinician measurements. A near-normal temperature does not exclude serious infection in an infant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left (contralateral) casted foot: toes pink and warm, neurovascularly intact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal contralateral foot localises the problem to the right cast but does not lower the urgency of the affected side; the left foot must still be monitored.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Cast-induced threatened limb ischaemia with infected pressure ulcer
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Dusky cool toes + foul cast odour + inconsolable crying together behind a plaster cast constitute a limb-threatening emergency that mandates immediate cast removal in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-person cast removal (bivalving) with full neurovascular assessment of the limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step — relieves compression (usually restoring perfusion immediately) and exposes the wound. Cannot be done via telehealth; it is why emergency escalation is mandatory.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / microbiological culture of the pressure ulcer
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides culture-directed antibiotics for the infected ulcer — performed in person after cast removal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Assessment for osteomyelitis / deep infection (clinical +/- imaging) and infant sepsis screen if unwell
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: An infected ulcer over a bony prominence in a young infant warrants exclusion of deep infection and systemic involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home attempt to cut, loosen or remove the plaster cast by parents
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Dangerous and outside a caregiver's competence with plaster shears on a small infant; the correct action is emergency transport for professional removal, not home removal.
